answer: Communities became the center of survival for many areas. Communities organized awareness events and safer sex campaigns. UCSB was a part of this and participated by hosting multiple HIV/AIDS awareness weeks and campaigns. The activist fought misinformation through education.

One example of a local orgnazation s the. AIDS: CAP initiative. This was a local organization that raised money through donations and performances such as the Cabaret Benefit II and The Strong Heart performance. These events mixed entertainment with activism, and the funds raised helped fund care, counseling, and support services.

Another need for survival was support. During such a hard time, it is crucial to have a support system to help you get through it and keep spirits up. We see this firsthand from letters by organizers and attendees that reveal the emotional impact and community connection these events provided. A letter from a man named Micahel, who was diagnosed with aids, writes to an event organizer thanking him and sharing the news that he is no longer PWA.
